mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in over 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.8% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy, which increases discount rates and forces equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.25% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates a demand shock that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 58% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving manufacturing output and business confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns (accuracy < 50%).
